game_info.title: Pixel Hopper
game_info.concept:
Crossy Road is an endless arcade hopper where players control a character by tapping the screen to hop forward one square or swiping left/right to move one square sideways, navigating an infinitely scrolling, procedurally generated environment. The game features various obstacles like multi-lane roads with fast-moving vehicles, train tracks with trains, and rivers requiring jumps across logs or lily pads, with any collision resulting in instant game over. Players collect scattered coins during runs, which are then used to unlock over 300 unique characters, each potentially introducing new visual themes or worlds. Progression is driven by achieving higher distances to set new high scores and completing daily challenges for rewards, with a local same-device multiplayer mode allowing two players to compete simultaneously.

App Store URL: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/crossy-road/id924373886?uo=4

================================================================================
Full Description:
================================================================================

Why did the Chicken cross the road?
Why did the Pigeon leave THAT there?
Why did Unihorse eat all that candy?

Crossy Road is the 8-bit endless arcade hopper that started it all. Collect custom characters and navigate freeways, railroads, rivers and much more.

Crossy Road® is the #1 viral smash hit you’ll never stop playing.

FEATURES:
• CROSS ROADS—Cross roads, train tracks, and rivers - endlessly hop forever!
• SILLY CHARACTERS—Unlock and collect over 300 characters in our distinctive retro style.
• DISCOVER WORLDS—Have fun running, hopping and crossing all day with over 28 worlds to discover.
• DON’T DIE—Avoid (or enjoy) hilarious ways to die as you cross through oceans, savannahs, space and more!
• LEADERBOARDS—Peck your way to the top in our daily challenges! Play around the world, win gifts and unlock rare characters.
• SHINY CARDS—Track your stats with special holographic cards that come with each character.
• SPECIAL EVENTS—Celebrate special dates with limited time events and free character giveaways.

EXTRA FUN:
• Jump into the action and enjoy innovative and simple gameplay.
• Discover and explore 28 different worlds each with their own unique arcade style.
• Complete Daily Quests! Hop endlessly, scare birds, jump over lily pads, and more!
• Same device multiplayer! Challenge your friends and family on same-device multiplayer mode.
• Free to play
• Play offline games 
• Join over 250 MILLION players worldwide

SUPPORT:
Have any problems or suggestions? You can reach us at support@hipsterwhale.com or check out our FAQ www.hipsterwhale.com/crossy-road-support
game_info.description: Welcome to **Pixel Hopper**! Navigate an endless, procedurally generated world filled with busy roads, treacherous rivers, and dangerous train tracks. You control a voxel character hopping forward to score points. Time your jumps carefully to avoid speeding cars and trains, and balance on floating logs to cross rivers. Don't stay still for too long, or the eagle might catch you! How far can you go?
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move the character (Up, Down, Left, Right)
Enter: Start the game from the main menu
Esc: Pause or Unpause the game
R: Restart the game after Game Over

TEST_1.test_description: Testing the safety heuristic and pathfinding algorithm.
TEST_1.strategy_description: The automated bot analyzes the lane ahead for obstacles (cars, trees) and safe platforms (logs). It calculates time-to-impact for dynamic objects and moves forward only when a safe window is detected. If the path ahead is blocked, it waits or moves sideways to find an opening.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The player character should consistently progress forward, successfully crossing roads and rivers without colliding, achieving a score of at least 20 within the testing duration.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing random input handling and collision system robustness.
TEST_2.strategy_description: The bot sends random directional inputs (Up, Down, Left, Right) at random intervals. This stresses the movement interpolation system and verifies that collision detection works accurately even with erratic movement.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The game should handle the chaotic inputs without crashing or glitching. The player will eventually die (expected behavior), triggering the Game Over phase correctly.
